negative_cases.why_wrong: Zi Np Discrete Filter: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Apply zi_np state updates across the transient run.

Zi Np Discrete Filter: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use zi_np() for discrete-time numerator/pole filtering fail.
Zi Np Discrete Filter: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Zi Np Discrete Filter: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Apply zi_np state updates across the transient run fails.

Zi Np Discrete Filter: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use zi_np() for discrete-time numerator/pole filtering fail.
